name: Update Existing Command Flags

tasks.title: Add missing flags to wolfcastle task add spec section
tasks.description: Add missing flags to wolfcastle task add spec section
tasks.body:
The task add command in cmd/task/add.go registers 10 flags not documented in the spec. Update the spec's task add section (line ~777) to include:

1. --body (string): Detailed task description/body
2. --stdin (bool): Read task body from stdin
3. --deliverable (string slice, repeatable): Expected output file
4. --type (string): Task type enum (discovery, spec, adr, implementation, integration, cleanup)
5. --class (string): Task class override (e.g., lang-go)
6. --constraint (string slice, repeatable): What not to do
7. --acceptance (string slice, repeatable): Acceptance criterion
8. --reference (string slice, repeatable): Reference material path
9. --integration (string): How this task connects to other work
10. --parent (string): Parent task ID for hierarchical decomposition

Update the Arguments and Flags table, Behavior section (explain how each flag is stored in state.json), Output section (show deliverables in output), and Examples section. The Synopsis should also be updated to show available flags.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 flags appear in the Arguments and Flags table with correct types and defaults
Behavior section explains body/stdin precedence and task type validation

tasks.title: Add missing flags to wolfcastle project create spec section
tasks.description: Add missing flags to wolfcastle project create spec section
tasks.body:
The project create command in cmd/project/create.go registers 3 flags not documented in the spec. Update the spec's project create section (line ~1381) to include:

1. --type (string): Node type, either 'leaf' or 'orchestrator'. Defaults to leaf. The current spec says the node is always an orchestrator, which is incorrect; the --type flag controls this.
2. --description (string): Project description text (written to the project .md file)
3. --scope (string): Scope description for orchestrator nodes (used by the planning agent)

Update the Arguments and Flags table, Behavior section (explain how --type affects node creation, how --description populates the .md file, how --scope is stored), Output section (include type in output), and fix the Description paragraph that currently says 'The new node is an orchestrator' to reflect that --type controls this.
acceptance_criteria: All 3 flags appear in the Arguments and Flags table
Description paragraph corrected to reflect --type flag
Behavior section explains leaf vs orchestrator creation

tasks.title: Add missing flags to wolfcastle spec create and wolfcastle status sections
tasks.description: Add missing flags to wolfcastle spec create and wolfcastle status sections
tasks.body:
Two commands have minor flag gaps:

1. wolfcastle spec create (cmd/spec.go, line ~1561 in spec):
   - Missing --body (string): Spec body text to write instead of the template
   - Missing --stdin (bool): Read spec body from stdin
   Update the Arguments and Flags table, Behavior section, and add examples.

2. wolfcastle status (cmd/daemon/register.go, line ~329 in spec):
   - Missing --expand (bool, default false): Show completed nodes expanded (default: collapsed)
   Update the Arguments and Flags table and Behavior section to describe collapsed vs expanded display.
acceptance_criteria: spec create section includes --body and --stdin flags

tasks.title: Update spec header with correct command count and group list
tasks.description: Update spec header with correct command count and group list
tasks.body:
The spec header (line 3) says '39 commands organized into 6 groups' and lists the subcommand groups. After all additions:

1. Update the command count from 39 to the correct total (39 + 3 new commands = 42 leaf commands).
2. Add 'orchestrator' to the subcommand group list in the header paragraph.
3. Verify the count is accurate by counting all ## headers in the final document.

This task must run last, after all other spec changes are complete.
acceptance_criteria: Header command count matches actual number of leaf command sections
orchestrator appears in the subcommand group list

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Update Existing Command Flags is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify that the task add section documents --stdin overriding --body when both are provided, and that the --type flag validates against the enum (discovery, spec, adr, implementation, integration, cleanup)

breadcrumbs.text: Documented all 10 missing flags (--body, --stdin, --deliverable, --type, --class, --constraint, --acceptance, --reference, --integration, --parent) in the task add spec section with updated synopsis, flags table, behavior, output, exit codes, error cases, and examples

Documented all 3 missing flags (--type, --description, --scope) in the project create spec section with updated synopsis, flags table, description, behavior, output, exit codes, error cases, and examples
Documented --body and --stdin flags in spec create section, and --expand flag in status section, with updated synopses, flags tables, behavior steps, and examples
Updated spec header command count from 39 to 43 after verifying all ## wolfcastle sections in the document. The orchestrator subcommand group was already present in the list.

Audit reviewed 1 file (~400 lines changed across 4 commits): docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md. PASS. Verified all documented flags against source code (cmd/task/add.go, cmd/project/create.go, cmd/spec/create.go, cmd/daemon/status.go). All flag names, types, defaults, and validation sets match implementation. JSON output keys (action, address) confirmed correct. Command count 43 verified by grep. Two accepted notes: (1) exit code mapping is speculative (pre-existing convention, not introduced here). (2) docs/humans/ CLI docs are outdated and missing new flags (pre-existing, out of scope). Build passes, all tests pass, no formatting or vet issues. Quality: thorough, consistent documentation work with good source-code fidelity.

audit.result_summary: Documented 16 missing flags across 4 CLI commands in the spec: 10 flags on task add (--body, --stdin, --deliverable, --type, --class, --constraint, --acceptance, --reference, --integration, --parent), 3 flags on project create (--type, --description, --scope), 2 flags on spec create (--body, --stdin), and 1 flag on status (--expand). Each section received updated synopses, expanded flags tables, rewritten behavior steps, corrected JSON output examples, new exit codes, and additional usage examples. The header command count was corrected from 39 to 43. All changes verified against source code.

audit.objective: Verify all work in Update Existing Command Flags is complete and correct
audit.what_happened: Reviewed ~400 lines of spec changes across 4 commits, all in docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md. Verified every documented flag against source code in cmd/task/add.go, cmd/project/create.go, cmd/spec/create.go, and cmd/daemon/status.go. All flag names, types, defaults, validation sets, JSON output keys, and command count match implementation. Build, tests, format, and vet all pass.
went_well: Flag-by-flag source code verification caught zero discrepancies, confirming high fidelity between spec and implementation
Consistent documentation style across all four tasks made the audit straightforward
improvements: Exit code mapping in the spec is inferred from convention rather than explicit code, but this is a pre-existing pattern not introduced by this node
action_items: Update docs/humans/cli/ files to reflect the new flags (escalated to parent node)

task-0001.objective: Document the 10 undocumented flags on wolfcastle task add in the CLI command spec
task-0001.what_happened: Updated docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md at the task add section (line ~777). Rewrote the synopsis to show all flags, expanded the flags table from 2 rows to 12, rewrote the behavior section to cover body/stdin precedence, type validation, parent task decomposition, and state.json field mapping. Updated output to match actual code (task_add action, deliverables key). Added exit code 5 for invalid type, added error case for invalid type, and expanded examples from 2 to 7 covering --body, --stdin, --deliverable, --type, --constraint, --acceptance, and --parent.
went_well: The task amend section already documented similar flags, so the style was consistent
Reading cmd/task/add.go gave exact field names and validation logic to match spec to implementation
improvements: The output JSON in the spec uses the key 'description' but the code uses the same; should verify the actual JSON key names match state.Task field tags if they drift
action_items: Verify other task subcommands (block, deliverable) also have complete flag documentation

task-0002.objective: Document the 3 undocumented flags on wolfcastle project create in the CLI command spec
task-0002.what_happened: Updated the project create section in docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md. Rewrote the synopsis to show all flags. Expanded the flags table from 2 rows to 5 (added --type, --description, --scope). Corrected the description paragraph that incorrectly stated all new nodes are orchestrators. Rewrote the behavior section to cover --type validation, leaf auto-promotion of parent nodes, scope fallback logic for orchestrators, description .md file generation, audit task template for leaves, and overlap advisory. Fixed the output JSON to match the actual code (action key is project_create not project_created, uses address not node). Added exit code 5 for invalid type. Expanded examples from 2 to 5 covering --type, --description, and --scope. Updated error cases to match actual error message strings from the source.
went_well: The source code in cmd/project/create.go was self-contained, all flag registration and behavior in one file
Prior task AAR established a consistent style for flag documentation that made this section straightforward to write
improvements: The error code mapping (exit codes 2-5) is inferred from the spec convention rather than explicit code; the Go code uses cobra error returns without numeric exit codes, so the mapping is speculative
action_items: Verify the remaining project subcommands (if any) have complete flag documentation

task-0003.objective: Document the missing --body and --stdin flags on spec create, and the missing --expand flag on status
task-0003.what_happened: Updated docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md in two sections. For spec create: rewrote the synopsis to include --body and --stdin, added both flags to the flags table with descriptions of precedence behavior, rewrote the behavior section to describe the three-way content assembly (stdin > body > placeholder), and added three new examples covering --body, --stdin, and the stdin+node combination. For status: added --expand to the synopsis and flags table with a description of the collapsed-vs-expanded display behavior, inserted a new behavior step describing the collapsing logic for completed nodes and parent tasks, and added two examples showing --expand alone and combined with --node.
went_well: The source code for both commands was straightforward: spec create has a clean stdin/body/placeholder precedence chain, and status threads --expand through a single boolean
Prior task AARs established a consistent documentation style that kept both sections uniform
improvements: The spec create error cases table still only lists two errors; --stdin read failure is not documented as a distinct error case because the Go code does not surface it separately
action_items: Verify spec link and spec list also have complete flag documentation

task-0004.objective: Update the CLI spec header to reflect the correct command count and subcommand group list
task-0004.what_happened: Counted all 43 '## wolfcastle' sections in the spec document. Updated the header from '39 leaf commands' to '43 leaf commands'. The orchestrator subcommand group was already listed in the header (likely added in a prior task), so no change was needed there. The task description predicted 42 (39+3), but the actual count is 43, suggesting the original 39 was already undercounting by one.
went_well: Grep-based verification gave an exact, auditable count rather than relying on arithmetic
improvements: None; this was a one-line change with clear verification
